title: The Oregon Trail
author: Francis Parkman
content: “The path was hard, but the destination was inevitable.”
action: Commit to the route regardless of the cost.

title: Roughing It
author: Mark Twain
content: “The time has come to stop talking and start doing.”
action: End the analysis; begin the execution.

title: The Wilderness Hunter
author: Theodore Roosevelt
content: “The credit belongs to the man who is actually in the arena.”
action: Enter the conflict you are currently avoiding.

title: Two Years Before the Mast
author: Richard Henry Dana Jr.
content: “There is no substitute for hard work.”
action: Double your labor on the essential task.

title: Autobiography
author: Davy Crockett
content: “Be always sure you are right—then go ahead.”
